# Source Card: Use of World Health Organization and CDC Growth Charts for Children Aged 0-59 Months in the United States

## Summary
This 2010 CDC MMWR Recommendations and Reports guideline (prepared with NIH and AAP input) establishes the US chart-selection rule for pediatric growth assessment: use the 2006 WHO international growth **standard** charts for children aged <24 months and the 2000 CDC growth **reference** charts for persons aged 2-19 years, transitioning at 24 months. It fixes the WHO-chart screening cutoffs at the 2.3rd and 97.7th percentiles (equivalent to +/-2 SD; labeled 2nd/98th on the printed charts) rather than the 5th/95th used with CDC charts, and documents that cutoff choice and chart set are population-specific and must not be mixed.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) Chart-selection by age band — locator: Summary + Recommendations — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:a0a73be05619844a56f84038b1839d47f17920fbd54ba40f8772b32897e29453]"
- (ev_002) WHO screening cutoffs = 2.3rd/97.7th percentiles (+/-2 SD; labeled 2nd/98th) — locator: Recommendations > Use of WHO Growth Charts for Children Aged <24 Months — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:cb400b30b5cd1b39636f6ea2489f76ffc08ebfe407fc686b3fff60db03f90850]"
- (ev_003) Standard vs reference distinction — locator: Growth Reference Versus Growth Standard — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:8443d632279b26698abec6fcc159c7d51d75e0c3a6954f9b9ba70a0d3ae7a5bd]"
- (ev_004) WHO reference population fully breastfed — locator: Breastfeeding and Growth Patterns — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:2aa4d06ce0d9e50892a56e82bfc6266a1c8ad1035176b60204bd74f87e1d08a9]"
- (ev_005) Low weight-for-age prevalence differs by chart (ages 6-23 mo) — locator: Selection of Percentiles — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:a8355d44dac07032cce6b9adff4b82f84d08776a88cb4d8ba6c67cb6e29b1d16]"
- (ev_006) Mismatched cutoff mislabels 10% — locator: Selection of Percentiles — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:226d238dd81a1d8c3a1e2fc721f0b3c82a915a7090fe5123607cdfe6a18704d6]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population scope: children aged 0-59 months, US clinical settings; the WHO-vs-CDC split is at 24 months (WHO <24 mo; CDC 2-19 y with a 24-35 mo transition window).
- "Assay"/method dependence: this is anthropometric growth charting, not a laboratory assay. Values are strictly chart-set specific — WHO percentiles belong to WHO charts, CDC percentiles to CDC charts, and the two are not interchangeable (mixing produces the 10% miscategorization in ev_006).
- WHO charts (2006) derived from the WHO Multicentre Growth Reference Study (MGRS, 1997-2003, six international sites) via the LMS smoothing method; CDC charts (2000) derived from US birth records, NHANES I-III, and PedNSS.
- Lifecycle: effective 2010-09-10; guideline version MMWR 59(RR-9). Supersedes prior US practice of applying 2000 CDC charts to children <24 months. No retirement date stated in-source.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- The 2.3rd/97.7th percentile cutoffs (ev_002) function as a screening trigger: a value beyond +/-2 SD "might be indicative of adverse health conditions" and, per the source, should prompt the clinician to seek causes of poor growth — a screen-to-follow-up boundary, NOT a diagnosis. The source explicitly states the percentiles "are arbitrary and not based on health outcomes." No diagnostic, dosing, or treatment directive is defined here.

## Limitations & conflicts
- An erratum was published for this MMWR article (noted at the top of the CDC HTML); its corrections were not reviewed for this card. Downstream CDS use should reconcile against the erratum before treating any number as final.
- Chart-set conflict is preserved, not collapsed: the CDC 5th/95th and the WHO 2.3rd/97.7th cutoffs are DIFFERENT screening rules for DIFFERENT chart sets and populations; they must never be averaged or swapped. ev_005/ev_006 quantify the divergence rather than resolving it.
- Very-low-birth-weight infants (<1,500 g) were excluded from the CDC charts for children <36 months; the WHO MGRS excluded births <37 or >=42 weeks' gestation and multiple births — so neither chart is validated for preterm/VLBW populations. Threshold applicability to preterm/VLBW infants is therefore a GAP in this source; no preterm-specific cutoff is locatable here.
- Sex-specific cutoff values are not separately enumerated in the text passages captured (charts are sex-specific but the report states cutoffs as unified +/-2 SD across sexes); if a CDS needs sex-stratified numeric bounds, those live in the underlying WHO/CDC chart data files, not in this recommendation text.
- Head-circumference-for-age curve coverage differs (CDC <36 mo; WHO <60 mo) — a coverage boundary, not a numeric threshold conflict.
